summary: Recent updates in the session negotiation area of Firefox, particularly concerning the Session Description Protocol (SDP) used in WebRTC (the technology behind video calls), focused on improving memory management and ownership handling. By transitioning to **UniquePtr**, a smart pointer type in C++, the code now better manages the lifecycle of SDP attributes, reducing the risk of memory leaks and improving overall stability. This change enhances the performance and reliability of features that depend on SDP for negotiating media sessions, ensuring that video and audio connections are more robust and efficient _without resulting in visible changes_ for users.
